sourceMovie.title: The Ritual
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Ritual works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Exorcism of Emily Rose
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ritual clicked for you, The Exorcism of Emily Rose (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ritual clicked for you, The Conjuring (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ritual?
faq.answer: Try The Exorcism of Emily Rose, The Conjuring, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It—each shares DNA with The Ritual while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ritual?
faq.answer: The Ritual (2025) is best described as Horror, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ritual worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
